top_risks: Manual install instruction downloads and executes remote content
Skill command bypasses model reasoning with direct tool dispatch
Skill directly exposes a high-risk OpenClaw tool
Manual setup runs a remote script
Direct slash-command dispatch to a sensitive tool

findings.title: Remote download piped into shell
findings.explanation: A remote download is piped directly into a shell.
findings.why_openclaw_specific: This is a baseline scanner finding. OpenClaw-specific runtime modeling is deferred to Phase 4, but direct execution and credential exposure primitives still matter because skills can be installed and invoked inside real OpenClaw environments.
prerequisite_context: The matched text appears in the scanned file content after normalization.
analyst_notes: This rule is inherited baseline coverage and is intentionally kept separate from Phase 5 compound/path logic.
findings.remediation: Remove direct download-to-shell execution and require a reviewed local script or pinned artifact.

findings.title: Manual install instruction downloads and executes remote content
findings.explanation: The skill body includes a manual command that downloads content and executes it directly.
findings.why_openclaw_specific: In OpenClaw skills, documentation commands are part of the operator-facing install surface and can influence real setup behavior.
prerequisite_context: The install signal was extracted from structured metadata or high-confidence setup instructions.
analyst_notes: Install-chain analysis distinguishes metadata-driven install behavior from manual copy-paste setup guidance.
findings.remediation: Replace remote execution snippets with reviewed local scripts or pinned artifacts.

findings.title: Skill command bypasses model reasoning with direct tool dispatch
findings.explanation: The skill config requests direct tool dispatch instead of normal model-mediated invocation.
findings.why_openclaw_specific: OpenClaw supports slash-command style tool dispatch that can bypass model reasoning and approval shaping for skill execution.
prerequisite_context: The finding relies on parsed SKILL.md frontmatter and normalized invocation metadata.
analyst_notes: Invocation-policy findings are specific to OpenClaw command semantics, not generic markdown content.
findings.remediation: Require careful review of command-tool targets and prefer model-mediated invocation when possible.

findings.title: Skill directly exposes a high-risk OpenClaw tool
findings.explanation: The skill directly declares or strongly implies use of the `exec` tool.
findings.why_openclaw_specific: OpenClaw skills can expose or guide concrete tool usage through metadata and slash-command wiring rather than only through free-form text.
prerequisite_context: Tool reachability was inferred from command-tool, requires.config, or high-confidence body guidance.
analyst_notes: Phase 4/5 tool reachability remains a structured-plus-heuristic analysis, not full data flow.
findings.remediation: Limit direct tool exposure and prefer least-privilege tool choices.

attack_paths.title: Manual setup runs a remote script

steps.summary: The skill body instructs the operator to run a remote script.
Following the setup step executes content fetched at install time.

edges.rationale: The manual setup text already combines retrieval and execution.

attack_paths.explanation: A manual setup command still forms an install-time attack path when it directly executes remote content.
prerequisites: The operator follows the setup instruction.
attack_paths.why_openclaw_specific: OpenClaw skills often use SKILL.md as setup documentation, so manual install commands are part of the real operational surface.

attack_paths.title: Direct slash-command dispatch to a sensitive tool

steps.summary: The skill directly dispatches to a tool.
The tool is high-risk or state-changing.

edges.rationale: Direct dispatch bypasses normal model mediation and leads straight to tool use.

attack_paths.explanation: The skill can map operator invocation directly onto a sensitive tool surface.
prerequisites: The skill is invoked as a slash command or equivalent user-invocable action.
attack_paths.why_openclaw_specific: OpenClaw supports slash-command style direct tool dispatch for skills, making invocation metadata part of the security boundary.

path_explanations: install_remote_script_execution: A manual setup command still forms an install-time attack path when it directly executes remote content.
